diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Syncope triggered by standing is classic for orthostatic hypotension from volume depletion.
Any older-adult collapse is a red flag requiring cardiac exclusion (ECG not available via telehealth).
Cerebral hypoperfusion and metabolic derangement from volume depletion cause a global confusional state.
New confusion in a frail elder mandates a sepsis screen regardless of the leading diagnosis.
Confusion requires stroke exclusion; here it is global rather than focal.
Reduced, concentrated urine output signals renal hypoperfusion — the hallmark of pre-renal AKI.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intense thirst with markedly reduced oral intake

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Thirst with a blunted intake response is characteristic of age-related dehydration during heat stress.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound lethargy and calf muscle cramps

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fatigue and cramps are non-specific but consistent with volume depletion and electrolyte disturbance.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe orthostatic hypotension (clinician-measured lying-to-standing BP fall)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A large postural BP drop with a compensatory tachycardia confirms severe intravascular volume depletion — sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers this from the collapse and postural symptoms, not a measured value.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dry mucous membranes, furrowed tongue, decreased skin turgor (tenting), sunken eyes, absent axillary sweat

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The hydration examination signs; skin turgor and mucosal assessment require the clinician's hands (dry lips and sunken eyes may be video-observable, but tenting is not).

investigation_details.investigation_name: Urea, electrolytes and creatinine (U&E) with eGFR
investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated urea-to-creatinine ratio with reduced eGFR consistent with pre-renal AKI
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: AKI severity and hyperkalaemia risk go unrecognised; unsafe to restart diuretic/ACE inhibitor.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A disproportionate urea rise relative to creatinine is characteristic of a pre-renal (volume-responsive) picture — bloods unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Marked tachycardia with weak, thready peripheral pulse (clinician-observed)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Compensatory tachycardia with a thready pulse indicates significant volume depletion — sealed clinician finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Continued loop diuretic and ACE inhibitor during volume depletion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Loop diuretics enforce fluid loss and ACE inhibitors impair renal autoregulation — both drive and worsen pre-renal AKI in this setting.

symptoms.symptom_name: No chest pain, no palpitations, no focal neurological deficit, no head strike

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of chest pain/palpitations lowers but does not exclude a cardiac cause; ECG still required in person.
No focal deficit makes stroke less likely; the confusion is global/metabolic.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Severe hypovolaemia / dehydration with pre-renal AKI
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Orthostatic syncope + oliguria + a measured postural BP drop + an elevated urea:creatinine ratio together define severe volume depletion with renal hypoperfusion.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urea, electrolytes and creatinine (U&E) with eGFR
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies pre-renal AKI severity and hyperkalaemia risk and guides safe rehydration and medication decisions — obtainable only in person.
Excludes an arrhythmic/cardiac cause of syncope and screens for electrolyte-related changes; not obtainable via telehealth.
Confirms and quantifies orthostatic hypotension — requires in-person measurement.
Screens for a urinary source of infection contributing to confusion once urine is produced.
